raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2115 could transform California's approach to opioid addiction treatment by making methadone more accessible.",
    "Methadone is a highly effective medication that alleviates symptoms of opioid withdrawal.",
    "Bureaucratic barriers in California have hindered access to methadone treatment, contributing to increased opioid overdoses since 2019.",
    "California's current regulations on methadone are stricter than federal guidelines.",
    "AB 2115 allows doctors to prescribe up to 72 hours of take-home doses of methadone for the first time.",
    "AB 2115 increases the amount of methadone that a patient can take home from a clinic.",
    "AB 2115 permits non-methadone clinic doctors to perform federally required physical exams for patients seeking treatment.",
    "AB 2115 removes the requirement for patients to have one year of documented opioid usage before receiving treatment.",
    "AB 2115 eliminates the mandate for patients to participate in frequent counseling services.",
    "AB 2115 allows patients to be absent from treatment for up to 30 days without being removed from a program.",
    "AB 2115 gives physicians greater discretion in determining appropriate dosages of methadone for their patients."
  ]
}
```
